title: Agrostis novae-hollandiae
intro: Agrostis novae-hollandiae, commonly known as the New Holland bentgrass or redtop, is a perennial grass species native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis novae-hollandiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional herbal medicine to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
taxonomy: Agrostis novae-hollandiae is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae.
